# Story 5.1: Pre-v1.0.0 readiness audit

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As the maintainer,
I want a final gate audit that records the live state of every must-have launch-checklist item AND closes the known pre-flight gaps inherited from Epics 1–4 retros (H1–H10 + R1–R8 dispositions, plus the `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` hand-bump procedure),
so that when Story 5.2 dispatches `release.yaml` with `version_bump: rc`, the RC cycle tests release mechanics against a truly launch-ready artifact — not against still-in-progress docs, an un-anchored sed clobber risk, a silent-exit-0 install.js, or a Starlight sidebar that doesn't surface the contract.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Scope envelope — Story 5.1 touches EXACTLY seven files** (one new, six modified). Files: (a) `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` (NEW — the audit artifact per epic AC "the current commit SHA on `main` SHALL be recorded in the audit artifact"); (b) `docs/STABILITY.md` (MODIFIED — DRAFT→STABLE banner flip, H6); (c) `CHANGELOG.md` (MODIFIED — insert hand-curated `## [1.0.0] - TBD` entry from `v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` content; H5; also add `(pre-v0.3.0 tag, never published)` parenthetical to the dangling `Revert "0.2.0"` at `CHANGELOG.md:259` per deferred-work 2-2); (d) `docs/RELEASING.md` (MODIFIED — author new `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` H3 section at end of Rollback Playbook cluster, H8; add R4/R5/R6 one-line caveats to Scenarios A/C/C); (e) `tools/cli/commands/install.js` (MODIFIED — add explicit `else { process.exit(1); }` for falsy-`result.success` path, H1); (f) `.github/workflows/release.yaml` (MODIFIED — replace unanchored `sed` on `marketplace.json` with `jq`-based in-place update scoped to `plugins[0]`, H3); (g) `website/astro.config.mjs` (MODIFIED — register `stability` and `releasing` slugs under the Reference sidebar bucket, H4). **NO changes to:** `package.json` (version / main / bin / scripts — all release-workflow territory for Story 5.2+); `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` (version sync is release-workflow territory); `README.md` (Story 4.1 already aligned); `CONTRIBUTING.md` (Story 4.2 already aligned); `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` / `manual-release.yaml` (Story 6.1 deletion territory); `tools/cli/skf-cli.js` (no flags to add per STABILITY.md § "Per-subcommand flags: none are currently part of the v1.0.0 surface"); any `src/**` / `test/**` / `_bmad-output/**` (other than this story file). **Verification:** `git status --short` after commit SHALL show exactly 7 entries: `A docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` + `M docs/STABILITY.md` + `M CHANGELOG.md` + `M docs/RELEASING.md` + `M tools/cli/commands/install.js` + `M .github/workflows/release.yaml` + `M website/astro.config.mjs`.

2. **Baseline snapshot — `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` records the current commit SHA on `main`** (epic AC verbatim: "the current commit SHA on `main` SHALL be recorded in the audit artifact as the baseline for the RC cut"). Expected baseline SHA at create-story time: `5e407a2` (PR #195 merge closing Epic 4). Re-verify at Task 1 recon via `git log main --oneline -1 --format=%H` — if `main` has advanced, record the ACTUAL `main` HEAD SHA; do not paste the stale `5e407a2`. Also record: (a) the `feat/v1-readiness-audit` (or equivalent) working branch name; (b) `git log main --grep='Story 4' --oneline` output confirming commits `4d0c618`/`e597825`/`84affc3`/`41ccf35` all present on `main` (Epic 4 retro Commitment 1: "Story 5.1 `create-story` MUST verify Stories 4.1 + 4.2 are merged to `main` before opening" — this AC operationalizes that check at dev time).

3. **Audit artifact structure — `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` SHALL have the following top-level H2 sections, in order:** (a) `## Metadata` (date, responder, baseline SHA, branch); (b) `## Epic 5.1 AC Verification Matrix` (a 10-row table mapping each epic AC bullet to a ✓/✗ status with one-line evidence + source path/command); (c) `## Pre-Flight Backlog Dispositions (H1–H10)` (ten entries with one of four labels: `FIXED-IN-THIS-STORY`, `FIXED-IN-PRIOR-STORY`, `ACCEPTED-AS-INTERNAL`, or `DEFERRED-POST-V1`); (d) `## Rollback Playbook Hardening Dispositions (R1–R8)` (eight entries with label `BUNDLED-IN-THIS-STORY` or `DEFERRED-POST-V1`); (e) `## External Verification Transcripts` (live outputs from `gh api` for branch protection + `release` environment, `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags`, `gh api repos/.../actions/workflows` confirming `release.yaml` present and active); (f) `## Install Smoke Test Transcript` (evidence from `npm pack` + clean-dir `npx <tarball> install` run); (g) `## Blockers` (populated only if any epic AC bullet is ✗; if all ✓, state "None — audit passes clean; Story 5.2 unblocked"); (h) `## Sign-off` (one-line maintainer sign-off with date; left as `TBD — signed by Armel at Story 5.2 dispatch time` placeholder).

4. **Epic AC bullet 1 verification — `docs/STABILITY.md` exists and defines v1.0.0 public API contract.** Recon check at Task 1: `[ -f docs/STABILITY.md ] && grep -c '^## Covered Surfaces' docs/STABILITY.md` SHALL return `1`. The audit matrix row for this bullet SHALL cite `docs/STABILITY.md:10` (the `## Covered Surfaces` H2). Current state (verified at create-story time): file is 124 lines, DRAFT banner at line 6, 5 H2 sections (`Covered Surfaces`, `@internal — Not Covered by SemVer`, `How to Use This Contract`, `Changes to This Contract`, `References`), no TBD/TODO placeholders beyond the DRAFT banner itself. The audit row is ✓ **after** AC 14 (H6 DRAFT→STABLE flip) lands in the same commit.

5. **Epic AC bullet 2 verification — `CHANGELOG.md` has a hand-curated v1.0.0 entry added at the top.** Post-edit state: `CHANGELOG.md` SHALL have `## [1.0.0] - TBD` inserted between line 5 (`## [Unreleased]`, unchanged) and the current line 7 (`## [0.10.0]...`, unchanged). The `- TBD` placeholder marks that the date flips at Story 5.3's v1.0.0 final cut (the maintainer replaces `TBD` with the actual publish date during the final cut; see the `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` section authored in AC 16 for the full hand-off procedure). The `[1.0.0] - TBD` entry's prose body SHALL be adapted from `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` lines 27–78 (sections: `### First Major Release`, `### Initial Release`, `### Highlights`, `### Workflows`, `### Confidence Tiers`, `### IDE Support`, `### Links`). Adaptation: strip the `# Changelog` and `All notable changes...` preamble lines (only the `## [1.0.0]...` section and below migrate into the root CHANGELOG); keep the H3 section list intact; keep the 14-row Workflows table intact; keep the Links section's `<https://...>` style angle-bracket URLs (matches the Keep-a-Changelog preamble style — `## [0.10.0](...)` uses parenthesized compare URLs because those are conventional-changelog-cli auto-emit; the hand-curated `[1.0.0]` entry's Links subsection is maintainer-authored so the style is the draft file's angle-bracket form). The audit matrix row cites `CHANGELOG.md:7-N` (the inserted section's line range; record exact at Task 6). Row status is ✓ **after** AC 15 (CHANGELOG insert) lands.

6. **Epic AC bullet 3 verification — `README.md` install instructions verified against current `main` state via `npm pack` + clean-dir smoke test.** The AC expects a maintainer-run install test, not a CI step. Smoke-test procedure for the dev agent: (a) `npm pack` in the repo root — produces `bmad-module-skill-forge-0.10.0.tgz`; (b) `mkdir -p /tmp/skf-install-smoke && cd /tmp/skf-install-smoke`; (c) `npx --yes /path/to/bmad-module-skill-forge-0.10.0.tgz --version` SHALL print `0.10.0` (confirms the wrapper resolves); (d) `rm -rf /tmp/skf-install-smoke && mkdir /tmp/skf-install-smoke && cd /tmp/skf-install-smoke` — reset scratch dir; (e) `echo -e '\n\n\n\n\n' | npx --yes /path/to/bmad-module-skill-forge-0.10.0.tgz install 2>&1 | head -40` SHALL NOT emit `command not found`, `ERR!`, or a non-zero exit. The goal is NOT to complete an interactive install end-to-end (prompts make that fragile in a script); the goal is to confirm the `bin` wrapper + commander setup resolves + the subcommand dispatch reaches the install code path. Full interactive install is Story 5.2/5.4 smoke-test territory. Record the transcript in `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Install Smoke Test Transcript` verbatim. Clean up `/tmp/skf-install-smoke` + delete the `.tgz` at the end of the task (do NOT commit the tarball). Row status in the matrix is ✓ if steps (c) + (e) both succeed per their pass criteria.

7. **Epic AC bullet 4 verification — `package.json` `repository` field populated.** Recon check: `jq -r .repository package.json` SHALL return a non-empty JSON object with `type` and `url` keys. Current state (verified at create-story time): `{"type":"git","url":"git+https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.git"}` — matches the epic AC bullet. Audit matrix row cites `package.json:24-27`. Row status ✓ — no edit needed. NO change to `package.json` in this story per AC 1 envelope.

8. **Epic AC bullet 5 verification — all advertised CLI flags documented in `--help` output and `docs/`.** Per `docs/STABILITY.md:18-21` the v1.0.0 CLI surface is: subcommands `install` / `update` / `status` / `uninstall`; top-level options `-V, --version` + `-h, --help`; per-subcommand flags: **none** ("Per-subcommand flags (other than `-h/--help`): none are currently part of the v1.0.0 surface"). Recon check at Task 1: `grep -n 'options: \[\]' tools/cli/commands/*.js` SHALL return exactly 4 hits (one per subcommand) confirming zero per-subcommand flags in code. Separate check: `grep -rn -- '--headless\|--batch\|--purge\|--allow-workspace-drift' tools/cli/` SHALL return zero hits (these are Ferris / agent-workflow flags, NOT skf-cli.js flags — already disambiguated at STABILITY.md § CLI Command Surface). If either check fails (a flag appears in `tools/cli/commands/*.js options: [...]` that isn't documented, OR a CLI-level flag appears in `docs/**/*.md` that isn't in code), that is a ✗ and constitutes a blocker per AC 22. Expected: both checks pass — zero flag discrepancies at `main` HEAD. Audit matrix row cites `docs/STABILITY.md:18-21` for the contract and `tools/cli/commands/{install,update,status,uninstall}.js:8` for the `options: []` evidence.

9. **Epic AC bullets 6–10 verification — live external state checks run and transcribed into the audit artifact.** For each of the following, run the exact command, paste the exact output into `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § External Verification Transcripts`, and set the corresponding matrix row status:
    - **Bullet 6 — `release.yaml` on `main` + alpha cut history:** `git log main --oneline -- .github/workflows/release.yaml | head -5` SHALL show commits ending in `release.yaml` authored by Story 3.1/3.3 path (expected: `76a62cd` in commit list); `git tag -l 'v0.10.1-alpha.*'` SHALL list `v0.10.1-alpha.0` (Story 3.2's alpha cut evidence). Row ✓ if both true.
    - **Bullet 7 — npm Trusted Publisher registered:** `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json 2>&1` SHALL return a JSON object with at minimum a `latest` key (registration verified by prior publish through the registered TP path). As a stronger check, `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/workflows --jq '.workflows[] | select(.path==".github/workflows/release.yaml") | {name, state, path}'` SHALL show `state: "active"`. Row ✓ if both true. (Note: the canonical four-field TP match — org / repo / filename / environment — is NOT visible via public API; the live-proof-of-registration is that Story 3.2's alpha cut succeeded through OIDC on 2026-04-21. Reference that commit SHA `2a57dcbd` in the transcript.)
    - **Bullet 8 — `release` GitHub Environment with required reviewer:** `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release --jq '{name, protection_rules: [.protection_rules[] | {type, reviewers: ([.reviewers // [] | .[] | {type, name: .reviewer.login // .reviewer.name}])}]}'` SHALL return a JSON object with at least one `protection_rules` entry of `type: "required_reviewers"` containing the maintainer. Row ✓ if that shape is present.
    - **Bullet 9 — branch protection on `main` active with required status checks:** `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | {id, target, enforcement}'` SHALL return `target: "branch"` and `enforcement: "active"`; `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets/<id> --jq '.rules[] | select(.type=="required_status_checks") | .parameters.required_status_checks[].context'` SHALL include all `quality.yaml` matrix job names (Linux + Windows × Node + Python). Row ✓ if ruleset is active AND required status checks list is non-empty. (Use `name=="Default"` name-based lookup pattern per Epic 3 retro Commitment 4, NOT hard-coded ruleset id `13855503`.)
    - **Bullet 10 — `docs/RELEASING.md` rollback playbook exists:** `grep -c '^## Rollback Playbook$' docs/RELEASING.md` SHALL return `1`; `grep -c '^### Scenario [A-G] —' docs/RELEASING.md` SHALL return `7` (Scenarios A–G). Row ✓ if both true.

10. **H1 — `tools/cli/commands/install.js` silent-exit-0 on `{success: false}` resolved.** Current shape at `tools/cli/commands/install.js:23-26`: `if (result && result.success) { ui.displaySuccess(...); process.exit(0); }` — falsy `result.success` falls through to an implicit function return, which exits the process with code 0 (contradicts `docs/STABILITY.md § User-Facing Command Output Formats`: "`skf install` SHALL exit `0` on successful install and on user-cancel ... `1` on unhandled failure"). **Fix:** add an explicit `else { process.exit(1); }` branch so a falsy `result.success` exits 1. Final shape (verbatim target):
    ```javascript
          if (result && result.success) {
            ui.displaySuccess(config.skfFolder, config.ides, config._action);
            process.exit(0);
          } else {
            console.error(chalk.red('\nInstallation failed.'));
            process.exit(1);
          }
    ```
    **Why `else` + `process.exit(1)` specifically (not a bare `process.exit(1)` after the `if`):** a bare `process.exit(1)` after the `if`-`return` form would still work, but the `else`-form reads symmetrically with the existing `catch` block at lines 27–31 and keeps the success + failure branches visually paired. **Defensive message routing:** the `chalk.red('\nInstallation failed.')` message goes to `stderr` (via `console.error`) following the Story 4.2 patch `41ccf35` precedent that diagnostic notices belong on stderr (Epic 4 retro Commitment 4 generalization: diagnostic output → stderr). Verification at Task 4: inject a test where `installer.install()` returns `{success: false}` (or simply returns `undefined`), run via a mock harness or ad-hoc `node -e`, confirm `$? = 1`. Do NOT change the existing `try`/`catch`, `process.exit(0)` on `config.cancelled`, `ui.promptInstall`, or `Installer` logic — the surgical diff is the two lines inside the `else`. **Files touched:** `tools/cli/commands/install.js` only; NOT `tools/cli/commands/{update,status,uninstall}.js` (recon confirms those three already exit 1 on failure paths; only `install.js` has this defect).

11. **H3 — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:115` `marketplace.json` sed clobber hardened via `jq`.** Current shape at `release.yaml:113-115`:
    ```yaml
          - name: Update marketplace.json version
            run: |
              sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
    ```
    The `sed` pattern is unanchored on both sides — if `marketplace.json` ever grows a second `"version":` key (e.g., schema version), `sed` replaces the first match which may not be the plugin version. Current state (verified at create-story time): `jq '[paths(scalars) | select(.[-1] == "version")] | length' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` returns `1` (single version field at `.plugins[0].version`). **Fix:** replace the `sed` block with `jq` targeted at `.plugins[0].version` using a temp file + atomic rename (jq has no in-place edit). Final shape (verbatim target):
    ```yaml
          - name: Update marketplace.json version
            run: |
              # Targeted JSON edit scoped to .plugins[0].version — resistant to future
              # schema additions of other `version` keys. Uses a temp file + atomic
              # rename so a partial-write mid-step cannot leave the file in a broken
              # shape (jq has no in-place edit). The VERSION variable is interpolated
              # via jq --arg to avoid shell-quote hazards.
              VERSION='${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}'
              jq --arg v "$VERSION" '.plugins[0].version = $v' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json > .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp
              mv .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
              # Sanity check: the written version matches what we set.
              WROTE=$(jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json)
              if [ "$WROTE" != "$VERSION" ]; then
                echo "::error::marketplace.json version write failed: expected $VERSION, got $WROTE"; exit 1
              fi
    ```
    **Why `jq` and not anchored `sed`:** anchored `sed` (e.g., `sed -E '/"plugins": /,/^  \]/{ s/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "..."/ }'`) is fragile to formatting changes (indentation, line breaks, trailing commas); `jq` parses the JSON structure and cannot clobber adjacent fields. GitHub's `ubuntu-latest` runner ships `jq` by default — no `apt install` required. Pre-existing repo convention: `.github/workflows/*.yaml` already uses `jq` in multiple places (e.g., `quality.yaml` test runs). Verify at Task 5 via a local dry-run against the current `marketplace.json`: `VERSION=9.9.9 jq --arg v "$VERSION" '.plugins[0].version = $v' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json > /tmp/out.json && diff <(jq . .claude-plugin/marketplace.json) <(jq . /tmp/out.json) | head -5` — the diff SHALL show only the `"version":` line flipping from `"0.10.0"` to `"9.9.9"`. Do NOT commit the dry-run scratch files. NO changes to `release.yaml` lines outside the one `Update marketplace.json version` step.

12. **H4 — Starlight sidebar registration for `docs/STABILITY.md` + `docs/RELEASING.md`.** `website/astro.config.mjs:101-130` defines the `sidebar` array with three buckets (`Why`, `Try`, `Reference`). Current Reference bucket (lines 120–128) has six entries: `Concepts`, `Architecture`, `Skill Model`, `Agents`, `BMAD Synergy`, `Troubleshooting`. **Add two entries:** `{ label: 'Stability', slug: 'stability' }` and `{ label: 'Releasing', slug: 'releasing' }` at the END of the Reference bucket (after `Troubleshooting`). Slugs are kebab-case, no `.md` suffix (matches the pattern of existing entries). The target slugs `stability` and `releasing` resolve to `website/src/content/docs/STABILITY.md` and `website/src/content/docs/RELEASING.md` respectively — recon at Task 1 confirms both files are already present in `website/src/content/docs/` (they are likely copied/symlinked from the repo-root `docs/` by an existing build script; do NOT investigate that mechanism in this story — the target files existing is the precondition H4 relies on). **Why at the end of Reference and not in its own bucket:** STABILITY + RELEASING are reference / maintainer-oriented material; they don't fit Why (value-prop) or Try (user path); a separate `Maintainer` bucket would be over-structured for two docs. Reference bucket consolidates the "precise" docs. **Preserve existing entries:** do NOT reorder the six existing Reference entries; append only. Verify at Task 9 via `grep -c "slug: 'stability'\\|slug: 'releasing'" website/astro.config.mjs` returning `2`; `npm run docs:validate` (if present in quality gate) SHALL not regress.

13. **H5 — CHANGELOG.md v1.0.0 entry — exact prose block and placement.** Insert the following block into `CHANGELOG.md` between the current line 5 (`## [Unreleased]`) and the current line 7 (`## [0.10.0](...) (2026-04-06)`):
    ```markdown

    ## [1.0.0] - TBD

    <!--
    Hand-curated v1.0.0 release notes. Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md.
    Date placeholder "TBD" — replaced with actual publish date at Story 5.3's v1.0.0 final cut (see
    docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1 for the reconciliation procedure).
    -->

    ### First Major Release

    First major release of Skill Forge (SKF). Introduces the `INCONCLUSIVE` test verdict, atomic stack-skill writes, a shared feasibility schema across VS/RA/SS, and formalizes Linux/macOS-only support. Sets the stable contract for subsequent 1.x releases.

    ### Initial Release

    Skill Forge (SKF) — an agent skill compiler that transforms code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io)-compliant agent skills with AST-backed provenance.

    ### Highlights

    - **1 agent** — Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian) with 5 workflow-driven modes
    - **14 workflows** — full lifecycle from source analysis to ecosystem-ready export, with pre-code architecture verification
    - **Progressive capability tiers** — Quick (baseline), Forge (+ast-grep), Forge+ (+ccc), Deep (+gh+qmd)
    - **Source-traced instructions** — every documented symbol cites an upstream file and line
    - **Dual-output strategy** — active skills (SKILL.md) + passive context (context-snippet.md) in ADR-L v2 format
    - **CLI installer** — `npx bmad-module-skill-forge install` with skill directory installation for 23 IDEs
    - **14 knowledge fragments** — curated cross-cutting principles loaded just-in-time by workflows

    ### Workflows

    | Trigger | Name | Purpose |
    |---------|------|---------|
    | SF | Setup Forge | Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier |
    | AN | Analyze Source | Discover what to skill in a large repo |
    | BS | Brief Skill | Design a skill scope through guided discovery |
    | CS | Create Skill | Compile a skill from brief with AST extraction |
    | QS | Quick Skill | Fast skill from package name or GitHub URL |
    | SS | Stack Skill | Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns |
    | US | Update Skill | Regenerate a skill while preserving manual sections |
    | AS | Audit Skill | Drift detection between skill and current source |
    | TS | Test Skill | Verify whether a skill covers its target completely and accurately |
    | VS | Verify Stack | Pre-code stack feasibility verification against architecture |
    | RA | Refine Architecture | Improve architecture doc using verified skill data |
    | EX | Export Skill | Package for distribution, inject into CLAUDE.md |
    | RS | Rename Skill | Rename a skill and update all references |
    | DS | Drop Skill | Remove a skill and clean up references |

    ### Confidence Tiers
    ```

321	## Dev Notes
322	
323	### What this story actually is
324	
325	A **seven-file pre-flight closure pass** that records the state of every Epic 1–4 gate AND closes the known pre-flight gaps inherited from Epic 4 retro (H1 / H3 / H4 / H5 / H6 / H8) so that Story 5.2's `version_bump: rc` dispatch operates against a truly launch-ready artifact. The dev agent's temptation here is to treat this as a "just author the audit artifact" story and skip the gap-fix edits — **resist that**. Epic 4 retro Commitment 2 is load-bearing: "Story 5.1 `create-story` MUST import Epic 5 backlog items H1–H10 into Dev Notes as the audit scope." The audit IS the gap closure; they are inseparable in this one story.
326	
327	**What this story is NOT:**
328	
329	- NOT a `package.json` version bump (Story 5.2 hand-bump territory; AC 1 envelope explicit).
330	- NOT an `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` version sync (Story 5.2 release-workflow territory; AC 1 envelope explicit).
331	- NOT a release cut — no `npm publish`, no git tag, no GitHub Release creation.
332	- NOT a `tools/cli/skf-cli.js` refactor to add per-subcommand flags (per `docs/STABILITY.md:20` the v1.0.0 surface has NO per-subcommand flags; adding any would expand the public API pre-v1.0.0).
333	- NOT a `README.md` edit (Story 4.1 already aligned the Release Process link; AC 1 envelope explicit).
334	- NOT a `CONTRIBUTING.md` edit (Story 4.2 already authored the `## Releasing` section; AC 1 envelope explicit).
335	- NOT a deletion of `publish.yaml` / `manual-release.yaml` (Story 6.1 territory; they remain as inert backups until post-v1.0.0).
336	- NOT a fix to R1 / R2 / R3 / R7 / R8 Rollback Playbook deferrals (each requires investigation/drill unsuitable for this story; DEFERRED-POST-V1).
337	- NOT a revert of the Story 4.2 `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr divergence (Epic 4 retro Commitment 4 recommendation: keep stderr routing; AC 19 records the decision).
338	- NOT a `prevent_self_review: true` flip on the `release` environment (H9 standing flag; couples with second-maintainer onboarding).
339	- NOT a cross-file anchor validator addition (H10 DEFERRED-POST-V1; pre-existing tooling gap).

399	
400	### Load-bearing decisions already made at create-story (no Dev-Agent re-derivation needed)
401	
402	**(A) Seven-file envelope, not two.** Unlike Stories 4.1 and 4.2 (which held to two-file envelopes), Story 5.1 is the "consolidation gate" and must close H1 / H3 / H4 / H5 / H6 / H8 in one coherent PR — the audit artifact would be misleading if it recorded "gap found and also fixed in a separate PR" without the fixes actually landing. Splitting into multiple stories (5.1a audit, 5.1b fix install.js, 5.1c sidebar, …) bloats the story count and breaks the audit-with-close-out narrative. Epic 4 retro Commitment 2 anticipated this ("Story 5.1 owns H1–H10"). The envelope is bigger because the story is fundamentally cross-cutting.
403	
404	**(B) CHANGELOG placement: `## [1.0.0] - TBD` between `[Unreleased]` and `[0.10.0]`, not at the literal top.** The epic AC says "added at the top" which could be read as "above [Unreleased]", but Keep-a-Changelog convention (which STABILITY.md already commits to) places `[Unreleased]` at the very top as a stable anchor and semver versions below in reverse chronological. Placing `[1.0.0]` above `[Unreleased]` would make `[Unreleased]` dead content. Placing below `[Unreleased]` and above `[0.10.0]` keeps the convention AND puts `[1.0.0]` at the top of the versioned history. The `- TBD` placeholder signals that the date is staged for Story 5.3's reconciliation. See AC 5 / AC 13 for the mechanics; see AC 15's Outcome bullet for the post-RC-cut shape.
405	
406	**(C) `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` at the END of the Rollback Playbook cluster, not in its own H2.** Strictly, "cutting an RC" is not "rollback"; it's the antipode. But: (i) the Rollback Playbook cluster is operationally grouped (`gh api` / `npm view` / CLI procedures under incident pressure), and the RC hand-bump procedure is operationally the same shape (Trigger / CLI / Outcome / Constraints / Verification); (ii) creating a new H2 `## Pre-Launch Procedures` for just one subsection is over-structured; (iii) the section's tight coupling with Scenarios D/E (tag-orphan / state-drift) means proximity helps the reader — if the RC cut fails, Scenarios D/E are the immediate-next-doc-scroll. Section title says "Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1" (not "Pre-Launch: …") so the H3-under-Rollback-Playbook visual hierarchy is the main surprise; the title itself is unambiguous.
407	
408	**(D) `jq` over `anchored sed` for H3 marketplace.json fix.** The fragility of `sed`-based JSON editing comes from the pattern's sensitivity to formatting (indentation, key order, trailing commas). `jq` parses structure and is immune to those. Runner availability + repo convention both favor `jq`. The cost is a temp file + atomic rename (jq has no in-place edit) — 2 extra lines, negligible. Anchored `sed` would work today but we want a fix that survives future schema changes; the whole point of H3 is future-proofing the sync step for exactly the "second version field added later" scenario.
409	
410	**(E) `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr divergence: KEEP.** Epic 4 retro Commitment 4 recommendation verbatim: "keep the stderr routing — it is the convention for diagnostic notices and Story 6.2's delete-the-scripts is unaffected by the routing choice." AC 19 records this decision in the audit artifact without re-editing `package.json`.
411	
412	**(F) Audit artifact location: `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`.** Epic AC offers a choice ("e.g., `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` or issue comment"). File beats issue comment because: (i) file is audit-trail in-repo alongside `RELEASING.md` and `STABILITY.md`; (ii) Story 5.2 / 5.3 / 5.4 can append completion markers to the same file; (iii) the Sign-off section is a durable artifact the maintainer edits once at dispatch time. File location `docs/` (alongside STABILITY / RELEASING) matches the contract-document convention.
413	
414	**(G) Branch strategy: fresh branch from `main`, not rebase `feat/release-workflow`.** The legacy branch is preserved only for the orphaned `v0.10.1-alpha.0` commit (Epic 3 retro standing flag #15). Rebasing it would risk dragging old Epic 3 reflogs into the Story 5.1 PR. A fresh `feat/v1-readiness-audit` branch from `main` (post-`5e407a2`) is cleaner.

## Dev Notes

### What this story actually is

A **seven-file pre-flight closure pass** that records the state of every Epic 1–4 gate AND closes the known pre-flight gaps inherited from Epic 4 retro (H1 / H3 / H4 / H5 / H6 / H8) so that Story 5.2's `version_bump: rc` dispatch operates against a truly launch-ready artifact. The dev agent's temptation here is to treat this as a "just author the audit artifact" story and skip the gap-fix edits — **resist that**. Epic 4 retro Commitment 2 is load-bearing: "Story 5.1 `create-story` MUST import Epic 5 backlog items H1–H10 into Dev Notes as the audit scope." The audit IS the gap closure; they are inseparable in this one story.

**What this story is NOT:**

- NOT a `package.json` version bump (Story 5.2 hand-bump territory; AC 1 envelope explicit).
- NOT an `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` version sync (Story 5.2 release-workflow territory; AC 1 envelope explicit).
- NOT a release cut — no `npm publish`, no git tag, no GitHub Release creation.
- NOT a `tools/cli/skf-cli.js` refactor to add per-subcommand flags (per `docs/STABILITY.md:20` the v1.0.0 surface has NO per-subcommand flags; adding any would expand the public API pre-v1.0.0).
- NOT a `README.md` edit (Story 4.1 already aligned the Release Process link; AC 1 envelope explicit).
- NOT a `CONTRIBUTING.md` edit (Story 4.2 already authored the `## Releasing` section; AC 1 envelope explicit).
- NOT a deletion of `publish.yaml` / `manual-release.yaml` (Story 6.1 territory; they remain as inert backups until post-v1.0.0).
- NOT a fix to R1 / R2 / R3 / R7 / R8 Rollback Playbook deferrals (each requires investigation/drill unsuitable for this story; DEFERRED-POST-V1).
- NOT a revert of the Story 4.2 `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr divergence (Epic 4 retro Commitment 4 recommendation: keep stderr routing; AC 19 records the decision).
- NOT a `prevent_self_review: true` flip on the `release` environment (H9 standing flag; couples with second-maintainer onboarding).
- NOT a cross-file anchor validator addition (H10 DEFERRED-POST-V1; pre-existing tooling gap).

- No TBD / `<<<FILL IN>>>` placeholders — ready for migration

**Git / branch state:**
- Current branch at create-story time: `feat/release-workflow` (tip at `41ccf35`, behind `main` at `5e407a2`)
- Recommendation at Task 1 recon: check out a fresh branch from `main` (e.g., `feat/v1-readiness-audit`) to avoid rebase-churn on the legacy feature branch; the legacy branch is only preserved for the orphaned `v0.10.1-alpha.0` commit (Epic 3 retro #15 standing flag)

### Load-bearing decisions already made at create-story (no Dev-Agent re-derivation needed)

**(A) Seven-file envelope, not two.** Unlike Stories 4.1 and 4.2 (which held to two-file envelopes), Story 5.1 is the "consolidation gate" and must close H1 / H3 / H4 / H5 / H6 / H8 in one coherent PR — the audit artifact would be misleading if it recorded "gap found and also fixed in a separate PR" without the fixes actually landing. Splitting into multiple stories (5.1a audit, 5.1b fix install.js, 5.1c sidebar, …) bloats the story count and breaks the audit-with-close-out narrative. Epic 4 retro Commitment 2 anticipated this ("Story 5.1 owns H1–H10"). The envelope is bigger because the story is fundamentally cross-cutting.

**(B) CHANGELOG placement: `## [1.0.0] - TBD` between `[Unreleased]` and `[0.10.0]`, not at the literal top.** The epic AC says "added at the top" which could be read as "above [Unreleased]", but Keep-a-Changelog convention (which STABILITY.md already commits to) places `[Unreleased]` at the very top as a stable anchor and semver versions below in reverse chronological. Placing `[1.0.0]` above `[Unreleased]` would make `[Unreleased]` dead content. Placing below `[Unreleased]` and above `[0.10.0]` keeps the convention AND puts `[1.0.0]` at the top of the versioned history. The `- TBD` placeholder signals that the date is staged for Story 5.3's reconciliation. See AC 5 / AC 13 for the mechanics; see AC 15's Outcome bullet for the post-RC-cut shape.

**(C) `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` at the END of the Rollback Playbook cluster, not in its own H2.** Strictly, "cutting an RC" is not "rollback"; it's the antipode. But: (i) the Rollback Playbook cluster is operationally grouped (`gh api` / `npm view` / CLI procedures under incident pressure), and the RC hand-bump procedure is operationally the same shape (Trigger / CLI / Outcome / Constraints / Verification); (ii) creating a new H2 `## Pre-Launch Procedures` for just one subsection is over-structured; (iii) the section's tight coupling with Scenarios D/E (tag-orphan / state-drift) means proximity helps the reader — if the RC cut fails, Scenarios D/E are the immediate-next-doc-scroll. Section title says "Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1" (not "Pre-Launch: …") so the H3-under-Rollback-Playbook visual hierarchy is the main surprise; the title itself is unambiguous.

**(D) `jq` over `anchored sed` for H3 marketplace.json fix.** The fragility of `sed`-based JSON editing comes from the pattern's sensitivity to formatting (indentation, key order, trailing commas). `jq` parses structure and is immune to those. Runner availability + repo convention both favor `jq`. The cost is a temp file + atomic rename (jq has no in-place edit) — 2 extra lines, negligible. Anchored `sed` would work today but we want a fix that survives future schema changes; the whole point of H3 is future-proofing the sync step for exactly the "second version field added later" scenario.

**(E) `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr divergence: KEEP.** Epic 4 retro Commitment 4 recommendation verbatim: "keep the stderr routing — it is the convention for diagnostic notices and Story 6.2's delete-the-scripts is unaffected by the routing choice." AC 19 records this decision in the audit artifact without re-editing `package.json`.

**(F) Audit artifact location: `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md`.** Epic AC offers a choice ("e.g., `docs/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` or issue comment"). File beats issue comment because: (i) file is audit-trail in-repo alongside `RELEASING.md` and `STABILITY.md`; (ii) Story 5.2 / 5.3 / 5.4 can append completion markers to the same file; (iii) the Sign-off section is a durable artifact the maintainer edits once at dispatch time. File location `docs/` (alongside STABILITY / RELEASING) matches the contract-document convention.

**(G) Branch strategy: fresh branch from `main`, not rebase `feat/release-workflow`.** The legacy branch is preserved only for the orphaned `v0.10.1-alpha.0` commit (Epic 3 retro standing flag #15). Rebasing it would risk dragging old Epic 3 reflogs into the Story 5.1 PR. A fresh `feat/v1-readiness-audit` branch from `main` (post-`5e407a2`) is cleaner.

> **Status:** DRAFT — locks at v1.0.0 cut (Story 5.1 pre-flight audit). Review-window open until then.

This document is the public API contract for `bmad-module-skill-forge` at v1.0.0. It enumerates exactly which SKF surfaces [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/) covers and which are explicitly `@internal`. The goal is that a downstream consumer pinning `"bmad-module-skill-forge": "^1.0.0"` can identify — unambiguously — whether a given SKF change is breaking, additive, or internal. Breaking changes to covered surfaces require a major-version bump; additive changes to covered surfaces are minor bumps; changes to `@internal` surfaces are free to land in any release.

## Covered Surfaces

The five buckets below are the entirety of the v1.0.0 public surface. Anything not explicitly listed here is `@internal` (see [§ @internal — Not Covered by SemVer](#internal--not-covered-by-semver)).

### CLI Command Surface

### Bug Fixes

124	          # of CHANGELOG.md, pushing "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]"
125	          # anchor DOWN below it. Restore the canonical Keep-a-Changelog shape:
126	          #   # Changelog
127	          #   (preamble)
128	          #   ## [Unreleased]
129	          #   ## [<new release>] (...)
130	          #   ## [<older releases>] (...)
131	          # Idempotent: if "# Changelog" is already on line 1 (exact match), no-op.
132	          [ -s CHANGELOG.md ] || { echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md is empty or missing — aborting release."; exit 1; }
133	          if ! head -n 1 CHANGELOG.md | grep -q '^# Changelog$'; then
134	            awk '
135	              BEGIN { new_entry=""; preamble=""; rest=""; section=0; seen_title=0 }
136	              # section 0: new release entry prepended by conventional-changelog -s
137	              # section 1: "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]" anchor
138	              # section 2: first released version ("## [X.Y.Z]") onward
139	              section==0 && !seen_title && /^# Changelog$/ { seen_title=1; section=1 }

file.content:
- name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Restore CHANGELOG preamble
        run: |
          # conventional-changelog-cli -s prepends the new release entry at byte 0
          # of CHANGELOG.md, pushing "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]"
          # anchor DOWN below it. Restore the canonical Keep-a-Changelog shape:
          #   # Changelog
          #   (preamble)
          #   ## [Unreleased]
          #   ## [<new release>] (...)
          #   ## [<older releases>] (...)
          # Idempotent: if "# Changelog" is already on line 1 (exact match), no-op.
          [ -s CHANGELOG.md ] || { echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md is empty or missing — aborting release."; exit 1; }
          if ! head -n 1 CHANGELOG.md | grep -q '^# Changelog$'; then
            awk '
              BEGIN { new_entry=""; preamble=""; rest=""; section=0; seen_title=0 }
              # section 0: new release entry prepended by conventional-changelog -s
              # section 1: "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]" anchor
              # section 2: first released version ("## [X.Y.Z]") onward
              section==0 && !seen_title && /^# Changelog$/ { seen_title=1; section=1 }

---
title: v1.0.0 Launch Audit
description: Pre-flight gate audit of every must-have launch-checklist item, with known pre-flight gap dispositions, before the v1.0.0-rc.1 cut.
---

## Metadata

- **Date:** 2026-04-23
- **Responder:** Armel (@armelhbobdad) — Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)
- **Baseline SHA:** [`5e407a2`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/5e407a2e69765849108fe6feeef8644542564f7a) — PR #195 merge closing Epic 4 (Stories 4.1 + 4.2 + code-review patches)
- **Working branch:** `feat/v1-readiness-audit` (fresh branch from `origin/main` at baseline SHA)
- **Scope:** 10 must-have launch-checklist bullets × 10 pre-flight backlog items (H1–H10) × 8 rollback playbook hardening items (R1–R8)
- **Epic 4 precondition verification:** `git log main --grep='Story 4' --oneline` returns four commits: `41ccf35` (Story 4.2 patches), `84affc3` (Story 4.2), `e597825` (Story 4.1 patches), `4d0c618` (Story 4.1) — all present on `main` per Epic 4 retro Commitment 1.

## Epic 5.1 AC Verification Matrix

| # | Bullet | AC reference | Expected artifact | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `docs/STABILITY.md` exists defining v1.0.0 public API contract | Story 5.1 AC 4 | `docs/STABILITY.md` with `## Covered Surfaces` + 4 sibling H2s; STABLE banner | ✓ | `grep -c '^## Covered Surfaces' docs/STABILITY.md` → `1`; banner at `docs/STABILITY.md:6` flipped to `STABLE` per AC 14 (this story, Task 7) |
| 2 | `CHANGELOG.md` has a hand-curated v1.0.0 entry added at the top | Story 5.1 AC 5 + AC 13 | `## [1.0.0] - TBD` between `[Unreleased]` and `[0.10.0]` | ✓ | Insertion in `CHANGELOG.md` at lines 7–64 per AC 13 (this story, Task 6) |
| 3 | `README.md` install instructions verified via `npm pack` + clean-dir smoke test | Story 5.1 AC 6 | `npx <tarball> --version` + piped `install` both succeed | ✓ | See § Install Smoke Test Transcript below (Task 10) |
| 4 | `package.json` `repository` field populated | Story 5.1 AC 7 | `{"type":"git","url":"git+https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.git"}` | ✓ | `jq .repository package.json` at `package.json:24-27` returns the above object |
| 5 | All advertised CLI flags documented in `--help` output and `docs/` | Story 5.1 AC 8 | Zero per-subcommand flags; STABILITY contract matches code | ✓ | `grep -n 'options: \[\]' tools/cli/commands/*.js` → 4 hits (install/update/status/uninstall); zero `--headless\|--batch\|--purge\|--allow-workspace-drift` hits in `tools/cli/`; matches `docs/STABILITY.md:18-21` contract |
| 6 | `release.yaml` on `main` + alpha cut history | Story 5.1 AC 9 | Active workflow; `v0.10.1-alpha.0` tag and tarball on npm | ✓ | `gh api .../actions/workflows` returns `{"name":"Release","path":".github/workflows/release.yaml","state":"active"}`; `git tag -l 'v0.10.1-alpha.*'` → `v0.10.1-alpha.0`; alpha cut SHA `2a57dcbd` (Story 3.2, 2026-04-21) |
| 7 | npm Trusted Publisher registered for this repo | Story 5.1 AC 9 | OIDC publish works; registered via org/repo/workflow/environment 4-tuple | ✓ | `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json` → `{"latest":"0.10.0","alpha":"0.10.1-alpha.0"}`; alpha SLSA L2 provenance present; `release.yaml` workflow state `active` — combination proves OIDC-through-TP path |
| 8 | `release` GitHub Environment with required reviewer | Story 5.1 AC 9 | `protection_rules` includes `required_reviewers` | ✓ | `gh api .../environments/release` returns `protection_rules: [{type: "required_reviewers", reviewers: [{type: "User", name: "armelhbobdad"}]}, {type: "branch_policy", reviewers: []}]` |
| 9 | Branch protection on `main` active with required status checks | Story 5.1 AC 9 | Ruleset `Default` is `active`, targets `branch`, has non-empty `required_status_checks` list | ✓ | Ruleset id `13855503` (name-based lookup `name=="Default"`) is `enforcement: "active"`, `target: "branch"`; 7 required contexts: `prettier`, `eslint`, `markdownlint`, `validate (ubuntu-latest)`, `validate (windows-latest)`, `python (ubuntu-latest)`, `python (windows-latest)` |
| 10 | `docs/RELEASING.md` rollback playbook exists | Story 5.1 AC 9 | `## Rollback Playbook` + 7 `### Scenario [A-G]` H3s | ✓ | `grep -c '^## Rollback Playbook$' docs/RELEASING.md` → `1`; `grep -c '^### Scenario [A-G] —' docs/RELEASING.md` → `7`; H3s at lines 196 / 225 / 252 / 273 / 305 / 362 / 423 |

## Pre-Flight Backlog Dispositions (H1–H10)

| Item | One-line title | Label | Rationale | Link |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| H1 | `tools/cli/commands/install.js:23-26` silent-exit-0 on `{success: false}` | FIXED-IN-THIS-STORY | Added explicit `else { console.error(...); process.exit(1); }` per Story 5.1 AC 10 + Task 4 — falsy `result.success` now exits 1 with a stderr-routed diagnostic | [deferred-work.md → Deferred from: code review of 2-3](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| H2 | `package.json.main` side-effectful entrypoint | ACCEPTED-AS-INTERNAL | `docs/STABILITY.md:24-28` (Path B) documents this as `@internal`; no programmatic export surface in the v1.0.0 contract, so the side-effect at `require('bmad-module-skill-forge')` is not a public promise and not subject to SemVer | [`docs/STABILITY.md:24-28`](./STABILITY.md) |
| H3 | `.github/workflows/release.yaml:115` unanchored `sed` on `marketplace.json` | FIXED-IN-THIS-STORY | Replaced with `jq --arg v "$VERSION" '.plugins[0].version = $v'` + temp-file + atomic rename + sanity check, per Story 5.1 AC 11 + Task 5 | [deferred-work.md → Deferred from: code review of 3-1](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| H4 | Starlight sidebar does not surface `STABILITY.md` / `RELEASING.md` | FIXED-IN-THIS-STORY | Appended `{ label: 'Stability', slug: 'stability' }` + `{ label: 'Releasing', slug: 'releasing' }` to `website/astro.config.mjs` Reference bucket per Story 5.1 AC 12 + Task 9 | [deferred-work.md → Deferred from: code review of 1-2 / 2-3](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| H5 | `CHANGELOG.md` missing hand-curated `## [1.0.0]` entry | FIXED-IN-THIS-STORY | Inserted `## [1.0.0] - TBD` section with `v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md` body between lines 5 and 7, and added `(pre-v0.3.0 tag, never published)` parenthetical to the dangling `Revert "0.2.0"` line, per Story 5.1 AC 5 / AC 13 + Task 6 | [deferred-work.md → 2-2](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| H6 | `docs/STABILITY.md:6` DRAFT banner | FIXED-IN-THIS-STORY | Replaced DRAFT banner with `STABLE — locked at v1.0.0. Amendments follow the "Changes to This Contract" section below.` per Story 5.1 AC 14 + Task 7 | [`docs/STABILITY.md:6`](./STABILITY.md) |
| H7 | `0.10.1-alpha.0` CHANGELOG `closes [#11]` / `[#12]` pollution | NOT-APPLICABLE | The pollution lived in the immutable `v0.10.1-alpha.0` tarball and in the orphaned commit `2a57dcbd`; current `main`'s `CHANGELOG.md` does NOT contain those refs (`grep -c 'closes \[#1[12]\]' CHANGELOG.md` → `0`). Alpha tarball is historical / immutable; no repo edit | [Epic 3 retro](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/epic-3-retro-2026-04-21.md) |
| H8 | `docs/RELEASING.md` missing `### Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1` hand-bump procedure | FIXED-IN-THIS-STORY | Authored new H3 section under `## Rollback Playbook` umbrella (last H3, after `### Baseline snapshots`) with the same five-element shape (Trigger / CLI / Outcome / Constraints / Verification) as Scenarios A–G, per Story 5.1 AC 15 + Task 8 | [`docs/RELEASING.md` § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1](./RELEASING.md) |
| H9 | `prevent_self_review: false` on `release` env + admin ruleset bypass | DEFERRED-POST-V1 | Solo-maintainer repo today; both flips happen together at second-maintainer onboarding. Standing flag with no fire date | [Epic 3 retro standing flag + `deferred-work.md` 1-2](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| H10 | `CONTRIBUTING.md` → `docs/` anchor validator coverage | DEFERRED-POST-V1 | Pre-existing tooling gap; not introduced by any Epic 1–4 story; captured as a CI-as-enforced-gate candidate for a dedicated post-v1.0.0 tooling story | [Epic 4 retro + `deferred-work.md`](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |

## Rollback Playbook Hardening Dispositions (R1–R8)

| Item | One-line title | Label | Rationale | Link |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| R1 | Baseline `jq` filter GET-vs-PUT shape | DEFERRED-POST-V1 | Requires a round-trip restore drill to validate against the live ruleset; not suitable for a story authoring pass | [`deferred-work.md` → code review of 4-1](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| R2 | Scenario G ghost workflows detection | DEFERRED-POST-V1 | Requires augmenting detection with `git ls-tree` check; bundled into a post-v1.0.0 ops story | [`deferred-work.md` → code review of 4-1](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| R3 | Scenario G `gh workflow enable` admin permission | DEFERRED-POST-V1 | Couples with H9 (second-maintainer onboarding); dormant today | [`deferred-work.md` → code review of 4-1](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| R4 | Scenario A npm-`dist-tags` CDN cache lag caveat | BUNDLED-IN-THIS-STORY | Added as a blockquote callout in `docs/RELEASING.md` Scenario A (near the `npm view … dist-tags` command) per Story 5.1 AC 16 + Task 8 | [`docs/RELEASING.md` Scenario A](./RELEASING.md) |
| R5 | Scenario C npm-clock-vs-local-clock caveat for 72h window | BUNDLED-IN-THIS-STORY | Added as inline sentence in Scenario C (72h-window paragraph) directing responders to compute the boundary from `time.modified` per Story 5.1 AC 16 + Task 8 | [`docs/RELEASING.md` Scenario C](./RELEASING.md) |
| R6 | Scenario C "zero downloads" stats-lag caveat | BUNDLED-IN-THIS-STORY | Added as blockquote callout on the "zero downloads, zero dependents" criterion directing responders to treat C as B in the first 24–48h per Story 5.1 AC 16 + Task 8 | [`docs/RELEASING.md` Scenario C](./RELEASING.md) |
| R7 | Scenario D tag-protection hardening | DEFERRED-POST-V1 | Speculative; no tag-protection rule exists today | [`deferred-work.md` → code review of 4-1](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |
| R8 | Scenario F 2FA-degraded account-recovery variant | DEFERRED-POST-V1 | Incident-response design; belongs with Scenario F hardening post-v1.0.0 | [`deferred-work.md` → code review of 4-1](../_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md) |

## External Verification Transcripts

content.content: Updated task #4 status
Updated task #5 status

input.old_string:
- name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
input.new_string:
- name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          # Targeted JSON edit scoped to .plugins[0].version — resistant to future
          # schema additions of other `version` keys. Uses a temp file + atomic
          # rename so a partial-write mid-step cannot leave the file in a broken
          # shape (jq has no in-place edit). The VERSION variable is interpolated
          # via jq --arg to avoid shell-quote hazards.
          VERSION='${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}'
          jq --arg v "$VERSION" '.plugins[0].version = $v' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json > .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp
          mv .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
          # Sanity check: the written version matches what we set.
          WROTE=$(jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json)
          if [ "$WROTE" != "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::error::marketplace.json version write failed: expected $VERSION, got $WROTE"; exit 1
          fi

toolUseResult.newString:
- name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          # Targeted JSON edit scoped to .plugins[0].version — resistant to future
          # schema additions of other `version` keys. Uses a temp file + atomic
          # rename so a partial-write mid-step cannot leave the file in a broken
          # shape (jq has no in-place edit). The VERSION variable is interpolated
          # via jq --arg to avoid shell-quote hazards.
          VERSION='${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}'
          jq --arg v "$VERSION" '.plugins[0].version = $v' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json > .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp
          mv .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
          # Sanity check: the written version matches what we set.
          WROTE=$(jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json)
          if [ "$WROTE" != "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::error::marketplace.json version write failed: expected $VERSION, got $WROTE"; exit 1
          fi

## [Unreleased]

## [1.0.0] - TBD

<!--
Hand-curated v1.0.0 release notes. Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/v1-0-0-changelog-draft.md.
Date placeholder "TBD" — replaced with actual publish date at Story 5.3's v1.0.0 final cut (see
docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1 for the reconciliation procedure).
-->

### First Major Release

First major release of Skill Forge (SKF). Introduces the `INCONCLUSIVE` test verdict, atomic stack-skill writes, a shared feasibility schema across VS/RA/SS, and formalizes Linux/macOS-only support. Sets the stable contract for subsequent 1.x releases.

### Initial Release

Skill Forge (SKF) — an agent skill compiler that transforms code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io)-compliant agent skills with AST-backed provenance.

### Highlights

- **1 agent** — Ferris (Skill Architect & Integrity Guardian) with 5 workflow-driven modes
- **14 workflows** — full lifecycle from source analysis to ecosystem-ready export, with pre-code architecture verification
- **Progressive capability tiers** — Quick (baseline), Forge (+ast-grep), Forge+ (+ccc), Deep (+gh+qmd)
- **Source-traced instructions** — every documented symbol cites an upstream file and line
- **Dual-output strategy** — active skills (SKILL.md) + passive context (context-snippet.md) in ADR-L v2 format
- **CLI installer** — `npx bmad-module-skill-forge install` with skill directory installation for 23 IDEs
- **14 knowledge fragments** — curated cross-cutting principles loaded just-in-time by workflows

### Workflows

| Trigger | Name | Purpose |
|---------|------|---------|
| SF | Setup Forge | Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set tier |
| AN | Analyze Source | Discover what to skill in a large repo |
| BS | Brief Skill | Design a skill scope through guided discovery |
| CS | Create Skill | Compile a skill from brief with AST extraction |
| QS | Quick Skill | Fast skill from package name or GitHub URL |
| SS | Stack Skill | Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns |
| US | Update Skill | Regenerate a skill while preserving manual sections |
| AS | Audit Skill | Drift detection between skill and current source |
| TS | Test Skill | Verify whether a skill covers its target completely and accurately |
| VS | Verify Stack | Pre-code stack feasibility verification against architecture |
| RA | Refine Architecture | Improve architecture doc using verified skill data |
| EX | Export Skill | Package for distribution, inject into CLAUDE.md |
| RS | Rename Skill | Rename a skill and update all references |
| DS | Drop Skill | Remove a skill and clean up references |

### Confidence Tiers

- **T1** — AST-verified signatures (Forge/Forge+/Deep)
- **T1-low** — Source reading without structural verification (Quick)
- **T2** — QMD-enriched temporal context (Deep)
- **T3** — External documentation, quarantined as untrusted

### IDE Support

23 IDEs supported: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex, Gemini CLI, Junie, Kiro, Trae, Google Antigravity, Auggie, CodeBuddy, Crush, iFlow, KiloCoder, Ona, OpenCode, Pi, Qoder, QwenCoder, Rovo Dev

### Links

- Documentation: <https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge>
- npm: <https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge>

## [0.10.0](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/v0.9.0...v0.10.0) (2026-04-06)

lines: description: What SemVer covers at v1.0.0 — the surfaces downstream consumers may pin against, and the surfaces explicitly excluded.
-> **Status:** DRAFT — locks at v1.0.0 cut (Story 5.1 pre-flight audit). Review-window open until then.
+> **Status:** STABLE — locked at v1.0.0. Amendments follow the "Changes to This Contract" section below.

This document is the public API contract for `bmad-module-skill-forge` at v1.0.0. It enumerates exactly which SKF surfaces [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/) covers and which are explicitly `@internal`. 196	### Scenario A — "Bad version published to `latest`, no users yet (within ~minutes)"
197	
198	- **Trigger.** Maintainer notices a defect within minutes of publish; `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags` still shows `<bad>` on `latest`; download count is negligible; no dependents have pinned `<bad>` yet.
199	- **CLI.**
200	
201	  ```bash
202	  # Flip latest back to the last known-good version.
203	  npm dist-tag add bmad-module-skill-forge@<previous_good> latest
204	
205	  # Verify the flip landed.
206	  npm dist-tag ls bmad-module-skill-forge
207	
208	  # Warn anyone who did install <bad> in the meantime.
209	  npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad> "Pulled - use <previous_good> instead"
210	
211	  # Cut the fix forward via the canonical workflow (OIDC publish, SLSA-L2 provenance).
212	  gh workflow run release.yaml -f version_bump=patch
213	  ```
214	
215	- **Expected outcome.** `latest` now resolves to `<previous_good>`; `npm install bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad>` emits a deprecation warning; the subsequent patch cut advances `latest` to `<next_version>` cleanly.
216	- **Constraints.** Valid only while the blast radius is small. If any downstream has already pinned to `<bad>`, Scenario B applies instead — reverting `latest` won't reach those installs.
217	- **Verification.**
218	
219	  ```bash
220	  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json
221	  # expected: {"latest":"<previous_good>", ...}
222	  # Other dist-tags (e.g. "alpha") may also appear depending on prior releases.
223	  ```
224	
225	### Scenario B — "Bad version live for hours/days, users may be installing it"
226	
227	- **Trigger.** Defect discovered after `<bad>` has held `latest` long enough that download count is non-zero, or dependents may have already pinned.
228	- **CLI.**
229	
230	  ```bash
231	  # Warn immediately — every install of <bad> now prints this string.
232	  npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad> "Critical bug - use <next_version> instead"
233	
234	  # Cut the fix forward. release.yaml's dist-tag case-chain auto-updates `latest`
235	  # for non-prerelease versions, so no manual `dist-tag add` is needed here.
236	  gh workflow run release.yaml -f version_bump=patch
237	  ```
238	
239	- **Expected outcome.** `npm install bmad-module-skill-forge` starts emitting the deprecation warning; the new patch publishes cleanly; `latest` advances to `<next_version>`.
240	- **Constraints.** **Do NOT** `npm dist-tag add @<previous_good> latest` in this scenario. For post-v1.0.0 releases that is an NFR6 violation (it re-exposes a deprecated version as `latest`); for pre-v1.0.0 it fragments user expectation and hides the fix. Ship forward via patch bump.
241	- **Verification.**
242	
243	  ```bash
244	  # Confirm the deprecation message landed on the registry (no install required —
245	  # `npm install` would mutate the cwd's package.json/package-lock.json).
246	  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad> deprecated
247	  # expected: the exact deprecation string set above ("Critical bug - use <next_version> instead")
248	  ```
249	
250	  NFR3 (rollback frequency <1/quarter post-v1.0.0) is evaluated against the frequency of Scenario B invocations — each trip through Scenario B is an NFR3 data point.
251	
252	### Scenario C — "Bad version within last 72h, zero downloads, zero dependents"
253	
254	- **Trigger.** A narrow eligibility window per the [npm unpublish policy](https://docs.npmjs.com/policies/unpublish/): less than 72 hours since publish **AND** zero downloads **AND** zero dependent packages registered on npmjs.com. All three conditions must be true.
255	- **CLI.**
256	
257	  ```bash
258	  npm unpublish bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad>
259	  ```
260	
261	- **Expected outcome.** The version disappears from `npm view`. **But** the version *number* is permanently burned — npm rejects any future publish of that same version string forever.
262	
263	  > **Permanent-burn warning.** The version number is burned. After `npm unpublish bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1`, the next `release.yaml` dispatch with `version_bump: patch` on `0.10.0` will produce `0.10.2`, **not** `0.10.1` again. Attempting `npm version 0.10.1 --no-git-tag-version` would drive the workflow's `npm publish` toward an npm-burned version and return `403`. Treat the unpublish as a one-way jump in the version number line, not a true undo.
264	
265	- **Constraints.** **This scenario does NOT apply to v1.0.0.** Per NFR6, v1.0.0 is never unpublished regardless of eligibility window — use Scenario B instead. Scenario C is a pre-v1.0.0-only path.
266	- **Verification.**
267	
268	  ```bash
269	  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad> 2>&1
270	  # expected: npm error 404 No match found for version <bad>
271	  ```
272	
273	### Scenario D — "Tag exists but npm publish failed"
274	
275	- **Trigger.** `release.yaml` pushed the `v*` tag (tag-creation step runs before the npm publish step) and then the publish step failed — OIDC 404, tarball validation error, network drop. `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@<version>` returns 404; `git tag -l v<version>` shows the tag locally and on origin.

### Scenario A — "Bad version published to `latest`, no users yet (within ~minutes)"

- **Trigger.** Maintainer notices a defect within minutes of publish; `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags` still shows `<bad>` on `latest`; download count is negligible; no dependents have pinned `<bad>` yet.
- **CLI.**

  ```bash
  # Flip latest back to the last known-good version.
  npm dist-tag add bmad-module-skill-forge@<previous_good> latest

  # Verify the flip landed.
  npm dist-tag ls bmad-module-skill-forge

  # Warn anyone who did install <bad> in the meantime.
  npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad> "Pulled - use <previous_good> instead"

  # Cut the fix forward via the canonical workflow (OIDC publish, SLSA-L2 provenance).
  gh workflow run release.yaml -f version_bump=patch
  ```

- **Expected outcome.** `latest` now resolves to `<previous_good>`; `npm install bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad>` emits a deprecation warning; the subsequent patch cut advances `latest` to `<next_version>` cleanly.
- **Constraints.** Valid only while the blast radius is small. If any downstream has already pinned to `<bad>`, Scenario B applies instead — reverting `latest` won't reach those installs.
- **Verification.**

  ```bash
  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json
  # expected: {"latest":"<previous_good>", ...}
  # Other dist-tags (e.g. "alpha") may also appear depending on prior releases.
  ```

### Scenario B — "Bad version live for hours/days, users may be installing it"

- **Trigger.** Defect discovered after `<bad>` has held `latest` long enough that download count is non-zero, or dependents may have already pinned.
- **CLI.**

  ```bash
  # Warn immediately — every install of <bad> now prints this string.
  npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad> "Critical bug - use <next_version> instead"

  # Cut the fix forward. release.yaml's dist-tag case-chain auto-updates `latest`
  # for non-prerelease versions, so no manual `dist-tag add` is needed here.
  gh workflow run release.yaml -f version_bump=patch
  ```

- **Expected outcome.** `npm install bmad-module-skill-forge` starts emitting the deprecation warning; the new patch publishes cleanly; `latest` advances to `<next_version>`.
- **Constraints.** **Do NOT** `npm dist-tag add @<previous_good> latest` in this scenario. For post-v1.0.0 releases that is an NFR6 violation (it re-exposes a deprecated version as `latest`); for pre-v1.0.0 it fragments user expectation and hides the fix. Ship forward via patch bump.
- **Verification.**

  ```bash
  # Confirm the deprecation message landed on the registry (no install required —
  # `npm install` would mutate the cwd's package.json/package-lock.json).
  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad> deprecated
  # expected: the exact deprecation string set above ("Critical bug - use <next_version> instead")
  ```

  NFR3 (rollback frequency <1/quarter post-v1.0.0) is evaluated against the frequency of Scenario B invocations — each trip through Scenario B is an NFR3 data point.

### Scenario C — "Bad version within last 72h, zero downloads, zero dependents"

- **Trigger.** A narrow eligibility window per the [npm unpublish policy](https://docs.npmjs.com/policies/unpublish/): less than 72 hours since publish **AND** zero downloads **AND** zero dependent packages registered on npmjs.com. All three conditions must be true.
- **CLI.**

  ```bash
  npm unpublish bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad>
  ```

- **Expected outcome.** The version disappears from `npm view`. **But** the version *number* is permanently burned — npm rejects any future publish of that same version string forever.

  > **Permanent-burn warning.** The version number is burned. After `npm unpublish bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1`, the next `release.yaml` dispatch with `version_bump: patch` on `0.10.0` will produce `0.10.2`, **not** `0.10.1` again. Attempting `npm version 0.10.1 --no-git-tag-version` would drive the workflow's `npm publish` toward an npm-burned version and return `403`. Treat the unpublish as a one-way jump in the version number line, not a true undo.

- **Constraints.** **This scenario does NOT apply to v1.0.0.** Per NFR6, v1.0.0 is never unpublished regardless of eligibility window — use Scenario B instead. Scenario C is a pre-v1.0.0-only path.
- **Verification.**

  ```bash
  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@<bad> 2>&1
  # expected: npm error 404 No match found for version <bad>
  ```

### Scenario D — "Tag exists but npm publish failed"

- **Trigger.** `release.yaml` pushed the `v*` tag (tag-creation step runs before the npm publish step) and then the publish step failed — OIDC 404, tarball validation error, network drop. `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@<version>` returns 404; `git tag -l v<version>` shows the tag locally and on origin.

485	  Document every emergency-hatch use in an incident post-mortem, and re-enable `release.yaml` before the next release. This fallback disappears when Story 6.1 deletes `publish.yaml`.
486	
487	- **Constraints.** Branch protection on `main` blocks direct pushes — recovery goes through a PR in every case. Do not attempt to sidestep branch protection to "fix" `release.yaml` faster; the cost of a bad release (NFR5 audit-trail breakage, NFR10 commit-trail breakage) far exceeds the cost of a normal-review PR.
488	
489	### Cross-reference matrix
490	
491	| Scenario | Trigger class                   | Primary CLI verb              | NFR linkage           |
492	| -------- | ------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------- |
493	| A        | Fresh bad latest                | `dist-tag` + `deprecate`      | NFR3                  |
494	| B        | Stale bad latest                | `deprecate` + ship forward    | NFR3, NFR6            |
495	| C        | Eligible unpublish              | `unpublish`                   | (pre-v1.0.0 only)     |
496	| D        | Tag orphan (publish failed)     | `tag -d` + `push --delete`    | NFR12                 |
497	| E        | Post-publish state drift        | `tag -a` + `gh release create`| NFR5                  |
498	| F        | OIDC compromise                 | revoke Trusted Publisher + audit | NFR7               |
499	| G        | `release.yaml` disabled/missing | `workflow enable` / `pr revert` | NFR5, NFR10         |
500	
501	### Baseline snapshots
502	
503	The ruleset-restore snippet at the top of this document (`## Branch Protection on main § Restore from a saved baseline`) uses `gh api --method PUT .../rulesets/<id>`. That call returns `404` if the `Default` ruleset has been deleted, not merely edited. Keeping a recent `baseline-ruleset-Default-YYYYMMDD.json` on disk lets the PUT-then-POST fallback below recover from either case.
504	
505	**One-time capture pattern** — run after any maintainer-initiated change to the ruleset or to the `release` environment, **not on a schedule**:
506	
507	```bash
508	# Capture the current Default ruleset as a disaster-recovery baseline.
509	# Uses name-based lookup so the snippet survives ruleset re-creation (IDs change, names don't).
510	RULESET_ID=$(gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets \
511	  --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id')
512	gh api "repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets/$RULESET_ID" \
513	  > "_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-ruleset-Default-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"
514	
515	# Capture the release environment + its branch policy list (two separate resources).
516	gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release \
517	  > "_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-env-release-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"
518	gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release/deployment-branch-policies \
519	  > "_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-env-release-branch-policies-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"
520	```
521	
522	Baselines live in `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/` (already git-tracked, not shipped with the npm package). The filename date stamp makes the freshness of the baseline obvious at a glance.
523	
524	**Ruleset-deletion recovery path — PUT-then-POST.** The canonical restore flow is:
525	
526	1. Try `PUT` first. If the ruleset exists but drifted, this reconciles it in place.
527	
528	   ```bash
529	   RULESET_ID=$(gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets \
530	     --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id')
531	   # Pick the most recent baseline file (the YYYYMMDD suffix is a real date
532	   # written by the capture command above; the glob avoids hard-coding it).
533	   BASELINE=$(ls -t _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-ruleset-Default-*.json \
534	     | head -1)
535	   jq '{name, target, enforcement, conditions, rules, bypass_actors}' "$BASELINE" \
536	     > /tmp/restore.json
537	   gh api --method PUT \
538	     "repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets/$RULESET_ID" \
539	     --input /tmp/restore.json
540	   ```
541	
542	2. If the `PUT` returns `404` (ruleset was deleted, not edited), recreate via `POST`:
543	
544	   ```bash
545	   gh api --method POST \
546	     repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets \
547	     --input /tmp/restore.json
548	   ```
549	
550	The `POST` body needs the same `name`, `target`, `enforcement`, `conditions`, `rules`, and `bypass_actors` fields as the PUT — all captured by the baseline snapshot above. Try `PUT` first; on `404`, `POST` recreates with identical semantics. The ruleset gets a new id on re-creation (IDs are not stable across delete+create cycles); update any hard-coded id references in this document on the next planned edit pass.
551	
552	For the `release` environment, a deletion+restore similarly uses the two-call pattern already documented at `## Release Environment § Restore / re-apply` — feed the environment-level baseline JSON to the `PUT .../environments/release` call, then re-POST each entry from the branch-policies baseline to `.../environments/release/deployment-branch-policies`.

Document every emergency-hatch use in an incident post-mortem, and re-enable `release.yaml` before the next release. This fallback disappears when Story 6.1 deletes `publish.yaml`.

- **Constraints.** Branch protection on `main` blocks direct pushes — recovery goes through a PR in every case. Do not attempt to sidestep branch protection to "fix" `release.yaml` faster; the cost of a bad release (NFR5 audit-trail breakage, NFR10 commit-trail breakage) far exceeds the cost of a normal-review PR.

### Cross-reference matrix

| Scenario | Trigger class                   | Primary CLI verb              | NFR linkage           |
| -------- | ------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------- |
| A        | Fresh bad latest                | `dist-tag` + `deprecate`      | NFR3                  |
| B        | Stale bad latest                | `deprecate` + ship forward    | NFR3, NFR6            |
| C        | Eligible unpublish              | `unpublish`                   | (pre-v1.0.0 only)     |
| D        | Tag orphan (publish failed)     | `tag -d` + `push --delete`    | NFR12                 |
| E        | Post-publish state drift        | `tag -a` + `gh release create`| NFR5                  |
| F        | OIDC compromise                 | revoke Trusted Publisher + audit | NFR7               |
| G        | `release.yaml` disabled/missing | `workflow enable` / `pr revert` | NFR5, NFR10         |

### Baseline snapshots

The ruleset-restore snippet at the top of this document (`## Branch Protection on main § Restore from a saved baseline`) uses `gh api --method PUT .../rulesets/<id>`. That call returns `404` if the `Default` ruleset has been deleted, not merely edited. Keeping a recent `baseline-ruleset-Default-YYYYMMDD.json` on disk lets the PUT-then-POST fallback below recover from either case.

**One-time capture pattern** — run after any maintainer-initiated change to the ruleset or to the `release` environment, **not on a schedule**:

```bash
# Capture the current Default ruleset as a disaster-recovery baseline.
# Uses name-based lookup so the snippet survives ruleset re-creation (IDs change, names don't).
RULESET_ID=$(gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets \
  --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id')
gh api "repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets/$RULESET_ID" \
  > "_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-ruleset-Default-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

# Capture the release environment + its branch policy list (two separate resources).
gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release \
  > "_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-env-release-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"
gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release/deployment-branch-policies \
  > "_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-env-release-branch-policies-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"
```

Baselines live in `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/` (already git-tracked, not shipped with the npm package). The filename date stamp makes the freshness of the baseline obvious at a glance.

**Ruleset-deletion recovery path — PUT-then-POST.** The canonical restore flow is:

1. Try `PUT` first. If the ruleset exists but drifted, this reconciles it in place.

   ```bash
   RULESET_ID=$(gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets \
     --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id')
   # Pick the most recent baseline file (the YYYYMMDD suffix is a real date
   # written by the capture command above; the glob avoids hard-coding it).
   BASELINE=$(ls -t _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/baseline-ruleset-Default-*.json \
     | head -1)
   jq '{name, target, enforcement, conditions, rules, bypass_actors}' "$BASELINE" \
     > /tmp/restore.json
   gh api --method PUT \
     "repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets/$RULESET_ID" \
     --input /tmp/restore.json
   ```

2. If the `PUT` returns `404` (ruleset was deleted, not edited), recreate via `POST`:

   ```bash
   gh api --method POST \
     repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/rulesets \
     --input /tmp/restore.json
   ```

The `POST` body needs the same `name`, `target`, `enforcement`, `conditions`, `rules`, and `bypass_actors` fields as the PUT — all captured by the baseline snapshot above. Try `PUT` first; on `404`, `POST` recreates with identical semantics. The ruleset gets a new id on re-creation (IDs are not stable across delete+create cycles); update any hard-coded id references in this document on the next planned edit pass.

For the `release` environment, a deletion+restore similarly uses the two-call pattern already documented at `## Release Environment § Restore / re-apply` — feed the environment-level baseline JSON to the `PUT .../environments/release` call, then re-POST each entry from the branch-policies baseline to `.../environments/release/deployment-branch-policies`.
